gaps.snippet: # [doc = " Guarantee strength for deadlock detection (complete for DAG channel graphs; cyclic = open)."] pub const DEADLOCK_DETECTION_STRENGTH : GuaranteeStrength = GuaranteeStrength :: Empirical ;

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " One non-blocking step of a communicating task."] # [derive (Debug , Clone , Copy , PartialEq , Eq)] pub enum Step { # [doc = " The task resolved (it will not be polled again)."] Done , # [doc = " The task made no terminal progress this step — typically parked on an empty/full channel."] # [doc = " **Never blocks**: parking is an explicit value, so the sweep can detect a stall (G2)."] Pending , }
gaps.reason: dropped non-doc attribute(s) on enum `Step`: # [derive (Debug , Clone , Copy , PartialEq , Eq)]

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " A communicating task that is **swept** (polled non-blockingly) rather than run to completion."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " `poll` must never block: park (return [`Step::Pending`]) instead of waiting on a channel, so a"] # [doc = " global stall is detectable as an explicit [`Deadlock`] rather than a hung thread (G2)."] pub trait PollTask { # [doc = " Take one non-blocking step. Returns [`Step::Done`] when the task has resolved."] fn poll (& mut self) -> Step ; }
gaps.reason: trait `PollTask` method `poll` signature has no confirmed mapping (a trait-body `Self`/`self` has no concrete referent in this grammar; `&mut self` conflicts with Mycelium's value semantics (ADR-003) — no correspondence)

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " The order a sweep visits still-pending tasks. Two directions over the same DAG network yield the"] # [doc = " same completion (Kahn-determinism, RFC-0008 §4.3) — the differential a test checks."] # [derive (Debug , Clone , Copy , Default , PartialEq , Eq)] pub enum SweepDir { # [doc = " Ascending task index (default)."] # [default] Ascending , # [doc = " Descending task index."] Descending , }
gaps.reason: dropped non-doc attribute(s) on enum `SweepDir`: # [derive (Debug , Clone , Copy , Default , PartialEq , Eq)]

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " A monotone progress counter for channel work — bump it on every successful `try_send`/`try_recv`"] # [doc = " so the sweep can tell \"a channel op advanced the network\" from \"nothing moved\" (G2)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " The driver reads it once per sweep; the caller increments it from inside task polls."] # [derive (Debug , Default)] pub struct Progress { epoch : AtomicU64 , }
gaps.reason: struct `Progress` uses named fields — no record/product-type surface (only a single-ctor positional shape maps to `type_item`)

gaps.snippet: impl Progress { # [doc = " A fresh counter at epoch 0."] # [must_use] pub fn new () -> Self { Progress { epoch : AtomicU64 :: new (0) , } } # [doc = " Record one unit of channel progress (a successful send or recv)."] pub fn bump (& self) { self . epoch . fetch_add (1 , Ordering :: SeqCst) ; } # [doc = " The current epoch (monotone)."] # [must_use] pub fn epoch (& self) -> u64 { self . epoch . load (Ordering :: SeqCst) } }
gaps.reason: no member of this impl block could be transpiled (3 sub-issue(s)): impl method `new` body: unsupported expression form `Progress { epoch : AtomicU64 :: new (0) , }`; impl method `bump` signature: function has no return type (implicit `()`) — no unit value is representable in this grammar fragment; impl method `epoch` body: unsupported expression form `self . epoch`

gaps.snippet: fn sweep_indices (n : usize , dir : SweepDir) -> Vec < usize > { match dir { SweepDir :: Ascending => (0 .. n) . collect () , SweepDir :: Descending => (0 .. n) . rev () . collect () , } }
gaps.reason: `usize` has a platform-dependent width; no fixed Binary{N} mapping

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " Sweep `tasks` cooperatively until all resolve, or return [`Deadlock`] on a no-progress sweep."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " `progress()` reports the monotone count of channel work done *outside* the tasks' own resolution."] # [doc = " A sweep counts as progress if **either** a task resolved **or** `progress()` advanced; a full"] # [doc = " sweep with neither, while tasks remain pending, is an explicit [`Deadlock`] — never a hang (G2)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Guarantee: schedule is **`Exact`** (a fixed function of `dir`); deadlock detection is"] # [doc = " **`Empirical`** (complete for DAG channel graphs — see module docs)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Errors"] # [doc = " Returns [`Deadlock`] (carrying the count of still-parked tasks) when a full sweep makes no"] # [doc = " progress while tasks remain pending."] pub fn run_dataflow (tasks : & mut [Box < dyn PollTask >] , dir : SweepDir , progress : impl Fn () -> u64 ,) -> Result < () , Deadlock > { let n = tasks . len () ; let mut done = vec ! [false ; n] ; let mut remaining = n ; let order = sweep_indices (n , dir) ; while remaining > 0 { let before = progress () ; let mut advanced = false ; for & i in & order { if done [i] { continue ; } if tasks [i] . poll () == Step :: Done { done [i] = true ; remaining -= 1 ; advanced = true ; } } if ! advanced && progress () == before && remaining > 0 { return Err (Deadlock :: new (remaining)) ; } } Ok (()) }
gaps.reason: unsupported Rust type form `& mut [Box < dyn PollTask >]`

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " As [`run_dataflow`], but each sweep's independent polls run **across the OS-thread pool**"] # [doc = " (M-709/M-864) — the \"checked across OS threads\" path (RFC-0008 §4.3 under the real scheduler)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Each still-pending task is polled **at most once per sweep** on a worker thread; the tasks are"] # [doc = " disjoint objects (no shared mutable state but the channels, RT1), so the parallel polls are"] # [doc = " data-race-free. The deadlock decision is identical and order-independent: a stall is a stall in"] # [doc = " any sweep order, so it is surfaced as the same explicit [`Deadlock`] (G2), never a hung worker."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " **M-864 note:** `Scheduler::run_indexed` now requires `'static` job closures (the persistent"] # [doc = " pool's worker threads outlive any single call), so this sweep can no longer *borrow* `tasks[i]`"] # [doc = " by `&mut` across the call the way the pre-M-864 `thread::scope`-backed scheduler allowed. Each"] # [doc = " still-pending task is instead swapped **out** of `tasks` (taking ownership, `'static` by"] # [doc = " construction — only the borrow was ever the obstacle, not the boxed task itself) for the"] # [doc = " duration of the parallel poll, and swapped back the moment its result is in hand — the sweep's"] # [doc = " own book-keeping (`done`/`remaining`) and the deadlock decision are otherwise unchanged."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Guarantee: deadlock detection **`Empirical`** (same basis as [`run_dataflow`], now exercised on"] # [doc = " real OS threads)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Errors"] # [doc = " Returns [`Deadlock`] when a full parallel sweep makes no progress while tasks remain pending."] pub fn run_dataflow_scheduled (scheduler : & Scheduler , tasks : & mut [Box < dyn PollTask + Send >] , progress : impl Fn () -> u64 ,) -> Result < () , Deadlock > { let n = tasks . len () ; let mut done = vec ! [false ; n] ; let mut remaining = n ; while remaining > 0 { let before = progress () ; let pending : Vec < usize > = (0 .. n) . filter (| & i | ! done [i]) . collect () ; let taken : Vec < Box < dyn PollTask + Send > > = pending . iter () . map (| & i | std :: mem :: replace (& mut tasks [i] , Box :: new (AlreadyDone))) . collect () ; let jobs : Vec < _ > = taken . into_iter () . map (| mut t | { move | | { let became_done = matches ! (t . poll () , Step :: Done) ; (t , became_done) } }) . collect () ; let results = scheduler . run_indexed (jobs , None , None) ; let mut advanced = false ; for (& i , (t , became_done)) in pending . iter () . zip (results) { tasks [i] = t ; if became_done { done [i] = true ; remaining -= 1 ; advanced = true ; } } if ! advanced && progress () == before && remaining > 0 { return Err (Deadlock :: new (remaining)) ; } } Ok (()) }
